chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-271 of 2026 (Sardar Khan v/s. P.O Sindh and others) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A.No. 1052/2026 (U/A). 2. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 3. For orders on M.A.No. 1053/2026 (E/A) 4. For hearing of main case. ------ 26.03.2026 Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Advocate for the Petitioner. ------ 1. Urgency granted. 2-4. Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner seeks release of certain amounts allegedly payable to him in respect of contracts awarded by the respondents in the year 2014. Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and perused the material available on record. From a bare reading of the petition, it transpires that the controversy relates to enforcement of alleged contractual obligations and claims arising therefrom. It is by now well-settled that disputes arising purely out of contractual obligations, particularly where determination of disputed questions of fact is involved, do not fall within the ambit of constitutional jurisdiction under Article 1 99 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Such matters are to be agitated before the competent civil forum or any other forum provided under the relevant law. Reliance is placed on 2026 SCMR 339 and 2025 SCMR 2081. In the present case, the petitioner seeks recovery of alleged contractual dues, which requires adjudication of factual controversies, examination of evidence, and determination of rights and liabilities inter se the parties—an exercise which cannot be undertaken in constitutional jurisdiction. No violation of any fundamental right or statutory duty in the nature of a public law obligation has been demonstrated so as to warrant interference by this Court.

In the present case, the petitioner seeks recovery of alleged contractual dues, which requires adjudication of factual controversies, examination of evidence, and determination of rights and liabilities inter se the parties—an exercise which cannot be undertaken in constitutional jurisdiction. No violation of any fundamental right or statutory duty in the nature of a public law obligation has been demonstrated so as to warrant interference by this Court. In view of the above, this petition is not maintainable and is hereby dismissed alongwith listed applications (if any). However, the petitioner is at liberty to avail any remedy available to him under the law before the competent forum. It is clarified that in case any such proceedings are initiated, the same shall be decided strictly in accordance with law, independently and without being influenced by any observation made herein, as this order has been passed only on the question of maintainability. Judge Judge Manzoor
